level_signals: Owns full payments platform vision and roadmap across billions in GMV

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: 10+ years of product management experience with significant focus on payments products and platforms (card/alternative payments, payouts, refunds, chargebacks, ledger systems).

Proven experience owning high-volume, mission-critical payments systems at marketplace, fintech or platform companies, including deep familiarity with processors like Stripe, Braintree, Adyen, or similar.
Strong product leadership, including setting strategy, influencing cross-functional partners, and driving complex initiatives from 0→1 and 1→N.
Deep understanding of financial data, reconciliation, and reporting, particularly supporting Finance, Accounting, and month-end close processes in high-transaction-volume environments.
Demonstrated ability to work in ambiguous, fast-paced environments, making sound decisions with imperfect information and balancing speed with risk and quality.
Proven track record using data and experimentation to inform product decisions, measure impact, and iterate on complex systems.
Strong product discovery and execution skills, translating multi-stakeholder problems into clear requirements and simple, intuitive experiences.
Excellent cross-functional communication, with experience partnering closely with Engineering, Design, Finance, Legal, CX/Ops, and risk-oriented teams.
High empathy for users and stakeholders, with passion for building trustworthy, transparent financial experiences.

nice_to_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Nice to have: Experience in a marketplace or multi-sided platform where payments, incentives, and marketplace dynamics are tightly coupled.

scope.surface: Core payments platform — payment initiation, processing, payouts, refunds, financial data infrastructure, incentive instruments; processing billions in annual GMV across 50+ categories
scope.business_model: marketplace / multi-sided platform (B2C and B2B sellers)
core_responsibilities: Own the product vision and roadmap for Payments, balancing short-term wins with long-term platform investments that support extremely fast growth and international expansion.

Deeply understand stakeholder needs – from sellers managing their businesses to Finance teams closing monthly books to CX resolving refund disputes; and translate them into clear product strategies and scalable systems.
Define and own core payments metrics including authorization rates, payout success, refund rates, processing costs, and financial accuracy.
Drive execution across multiple workstreams, coordinating backend payments systems, payment integrations, and frontend UX while managing dependencies and timelines.
Build the financial data foundation the business needs, including ledger systems that power internal reporting, seller transparency and tools they need, and accurate month-end closes.
Partner cross-functionally with Finance, Legal, Trust & Safety, CX, and Operations to ensure compliance, manage risk appropriately, and support efficient operations at scale.
Establish metrics and experimentation frameworks to validate performance, test new capabilities, and continuously improve outcomes for users and internal teams.

green_flags: Clear ownership scope: full payments stack across billions in GMV
Explicit metrics to own (authorization rates, payout success, refund rates, processing costs)
Remote co-located model with multiple hub city options (NY, SF, Seattle, LA)
Role reports to VP of Product — high visibility
Company named #1 Best Startup Employer in America by Forbes (Forbes claim in JD)
Explicit international expansion scope (50+ categories, global sellers)
red_flags: 10+ years requirement is a hard bar that eliminates mid-career candidates
Hub commute required (not fully remote) despite 'remote co-located' framing — potential ambiguity
Anomalous '1212' string at bottom of JD body — possible formatting artifact

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.level (inferred from 10+ years requirement, VP reporting line, and GMV ownership scope)
scope.business_model (inferred from marketplace/livestream commerce product description)
scope.primary_metrics_inferred (inferred from responsibilities; partially stated in JD)
location.office_time_required (inferred from 'within commuting distance' language; specific % not stated)
ambiguities: JD describes team as 'remote co-located' but simultaneously states candidates 'are required to be within commuting distance' of NY, SF, Seattle, or LA hubs — this is hybrid, not fully remote; treated as hybrid.
Anomalous '1212' string appears in JD body between the requirements section and the EOE section — likely a formatting artifact, ignored.
Visa sponsorship is not mentioned anywhere in the JD; treated as 'unclear'.
